title: IELTS Academic Mock Test 1
description: Complete IELTS Academic test with all 4 skills
instructions: Read the instructions for each part of the paper carefully. Answer all the questions. Write your answers in the answer sheet. You must complete the answer sheet within the time limit. At the end of the test, hand in both this question paper and your answer sheet.

words.definition: to become aware of, to sense
words.example: Fish perceive electrical signals in water.
The fish responds to electrical stimuli.

words.definition: a material that allows electricity to pass through
words.example: Water is an efficient conductor of electricity.

words.definition: an animal that hunts other animals for food
words.example: Sharks are top predators in the ocean.
The jamming avoidance response is a fascinating mechanism.
As the shark reaches proximity to its prey, it attacks.
A degree is a prerequisite for many jobs.
Academic autonomy allows universities to set their own curricula.
Farmers must adapt to changing weather.
Sustainable farming practices protect the soil.

content.text: Open your eyes in sea water and it is difficult to see much more than a murky, bleary green colour. Sounds, too, are garbled and difficult to comprehend. Without specialised equipment humans would be lost in these deep sea habitats, so how do fish make it seem so easy? Much of this is due to a biological phenomenon known as electroreception – the ability to perceive and act upon electrical stimuli as part of the overall senses.

Electroreception comes in two variants. While all animals generate electric signals because they are emitted by the nervous system, some animals have the ability – known as passive electroreception – to receive and decode electric signals generated by other animals in order to sense their location, size and even their health status.

Other creatures can go further still. Animals with active electroreception possess bodily organs that generate special electric signals on cue. These can be used for mating signals and territorial displays as well as locating objects in the water through a process similar to echolocation used by bats.

The best-known examples are sharks and their relatives, which possess specialised organs called ampullae of Lorenzini. These detect the tiny electrical fields produced by the muscle contractions of potential prey.
Research has shown that some species can even use electroreception for navigation. Migratory fish may detect the Earth's magnetic field, which is naturally accompanied by weak electric currents.

Interestingly, some freshwater fish have also developed this ability despite the lower conductivity of fresh water compared to salt water. This suggests that electroreception evolved independently in different species.
Scientists are now investigating potential applications of this phenomenon. Understanding how fish perceive electrical signals could lead to developments in medical imaging technology.

However, this remarkable ability also has its limitations. Strong artificial electrical fields, such as those produced by fishing equipment or pollution, can interfere with a fish's natural electroreception.

questions.text: how electroreception can be used for mating signals
a possible use for electroreception that will benefit humans
how fish can navigate during migration
the name of the organs sharks use for electroreception

questions.text: Electroreception is found mainly in ___ creatures.

subTasks.instruction: Do the following statements agree with the views of the writer?

questions.text: All animals have some degree of electroreception.
Freshwater fish cannot detect electrical signals.
Pollution can interfere with fish electroreception.

subTasks.instruction: Complete the sentences below. NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.

questions.text: Active electroreception can be used for locating objects through a process similar to ___ used by bats.
The Earth's magnetic field is accompanied by weak ___.

passages.title: The Role of Universities in Modern Society

content.text: Universities have traditionally been seen as institutions dedicated primarily to the transmission of knowledge and the preparation of students for professional careers. However, in recent decades, their role has expanded considerably to include research, community service, and economic development.

The modern university is expected to fulfill multiple functions. It must provide high-quality education while simultaneously producing cutting-edge research. This dual mission sometimes creates tensions, as faculty members must balance teaching responsibilities with research demands.

Another significant change has been the dramatic increase in university enrollment rates worldwide. What was once an elite privilege available to a small percentage of the population is now becoming a mass phenomenon.

Funding models have also evolved. While traditional public universities relied heavily on government subsidies, many now derive substantial revenue from private sources, including tuition fees, corporate partnerships, and research grants.

International collaboration has become increasingly important in higher education. Universities form partnerships with institutions abroad to share knowledge, exchange students, and conduct joint research projects.
Technology is transforming how universities deliver education. Online learning platforms, virtual classrooms, and massive open online courses (MOOCs) have expanded access to higher education.

Critics argue that the commercialization of higher education threatens academic integrity. When universities depend on corporate funding, they may be pressured to prioritize research that benefits their sponsors.
Despite these challenges, universities remain essential institutions for societal progress. They produce the skilled workers, innovative thinkers, and informed citizens that economies need.

Paragraph D

What three sources do universities now derive revenue from? (2)

passages.title: Climate Change Impacts on Global Agriculture

content.text: Climate change poses significant challenges to agricultural systems worldwide. Rising temperatures, changing precipitation patterns, and increasing frequency of extreme weather events are affecting crop yields and food security across the globe.

The impacts vary significantly by region. In some areas, warming temperatures may extend growing seasons and allow new crops to be cultivated. However, in many tropical and subtropical regions, higher temperatures combined with reduced rainfall are likely to decrease productivity.

Adaptation strategies include developing heat-tolerant crop varieties, improving irrigation efficiency, and diversifying agricultural systems. Additionally, farmers can adopt conservation agriculture practices that help soils retain moisture and nutrients.

Mitigation efforts in agriculture focus on reducing greenhouse gas emissions while also enhancing carbon sequestration in soils and biomass. Sustainable practices such as agroforestry, cover cropping, and improved fertilizer management can contribute to both climate mitigation and adaptation goals.
Water scarcity is becoming an increasing concern for agriculture. As temperatures rise, evaporation rates increase, and traditional water sources become less reliable.

The economic impacts of climate change on agriculture are profound. Farmers face increasing costs as they adapt to new conditions, purchase new equipment, and develop new skills. These costs can be particularly burdensome for small-scale farmers in developing countries.

Insurance mechanisms are emerging as important risk management tools. Crop insurance programs can help farmers recover from climate-related losses, but these schemes require government support to remain viable.

Ultimately, addressing climate change in agriculture requires coordinated global action. International agreements, technology transfer, and financial support for developing nations are essential components of an effective response.

subTasks.instruction: Complete the sentences below. NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS.

questions.text: Climate change affects ___ patterns and crop yields.
Tropical regions may experience ___ in productivity due to heat.
___ agriculture helps soils retain moisture.
Small-scale farmers face increasing ___ due to climate change.

sections.title: Task 1: Data Description
sections.instruction: You should spend about 20 minutes on this task. Write at least 150 words.
sections.prompt: Summarize the information by selecting and reporting the main features, and make comparisons where relevant.
bullets: Select and describe the main features

sections.title: Task 2: Essay
sections.instruction: You should spend about 40 minutes on this task. Write at least 250 words.
sections.prompt: Some people believe that unpaid community service should be a compulsory part of high school programs. To what extent do you agree or disagree?
bullets: Include relevant examples from your knowledge or experience

listening.title: Listening
listening.instructions: You will hear a number of different recordings and you will have to answer questions on what you hear.

sections.subtitle: Booking a Holiday Cottage
sections.description: You will hear a conversation between a travel agent and a customer who wants to book a holiday cottage.
sections.transcript:
Travel Agent: Good morning, Lake District Holidays. How can I help you?

Customer: Good morning. I'm interested in booking a holiday cottage in the Lake District.

Travel Agent: Of course. May I take your name?

Customer: Yes, it's Sarah Mitchell. S-A-R-A-H M-I-T-C-H-E-L-L.

Travel Agent: Thank you, Sarah. And may I have your telephone number?

Customer: It's 07788 456321.

Travel Agent: 07788 456321. Perfect. Now, do you have a preferred location in the Lake District?

Customer: We're happy to be anywhere in the Lake District, but we'd prefer somewhere quiet.

Travel Agent: I understand. How many people will be staying?

Customer: There will be four adults and two children.

Travel Agent: Four adults and two children. What's your budget per week?

Customer: We're looking at around 800 pounds per week maximum.

Travel Agent: And are there any facilities you particularly need?

Customer: Yes, we have a dog, so we need a garden.
sections.context: Conversation between two speakers - social situation
